title: [Bug]: Exponential retry backoff can overflow before applying the cap
summary: Very large retry attempts can raise an overflow before RetryPolicy applies the configured maximum backoff cap.
observed: An extreme delivery attempt in exponential mode can raise OverflowError while computing the uncapped delay.
expected: RetryPolicy should return the configured maximum delay for extreme exponential attempts instead of raising.
reproduction: Run the focused regression test that asks RetryPolicy for a very large exponential retry attempt.
impact: A malformed or unexpectedly high delivery attempt value can destabilize retry handling.
delivery_semantics: The fix keeps retry handling bounded and prefers redelivery over process failure.
security: Bounding arithmetic prevents hostile or corrupted metadata from triggering avoidable runtime errors.
acceptance: Extreme exponential attempts return the configured maximum backoff.
Normal exponential backoff values remain unchanged.
The focused regression test and full checks pass.
tests: Run the focused regression test, retry tests, runner failure-path tests, and the full local check script.
documentation: Update retry documentation or release notes if overflow hardening is mentioned.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published with regression and verification evidence.
